design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate overhead stages, defeat waves of Japanese mythological creatures using ofuda and gohei attacks, progress toward Black Mantle boss
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Deceptive cuteness masking extreme difficulty; contrast between shrine maiden protagonist aesthetic and punishing bullet patterns
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a Shinto shrine maiden warrior purifying the corrupted spirit realm alongside a loyal tanuki companion
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetically hard 2D shooter that deliberately subverts expectations through cute character design and presentation while delivering brutal challenge. Regional versions show design philosophy shift: Japanese version maintains unforgiving baseline, international versions offer tiered difficulty options (Easy/Hard) suggesting west-facing design compromise

difficulty.lives_system: JP version: 3 lives per credit across all difficulties. International versions: 5 lives on Easy, 2 lives on Hard
difficulty.continues_system: Credit/continue system present but not detailed in sources
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: International versions introduce selectable difficulty (Easy/Hard) with distinct resource allocations (lives and bomb counts). Japanese version enforces single difficulty curve. Bomb capacity increased from 3 (JP) to 4 (International), suggesting difficulty tuning via resource availability rather than enemy behavior changes

progression.upgrade_system: Bomb/explosive power-ups as consumable resources; ofuda and gohei as primary attack tools
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression linear through defeat of stage bosses; hidden level select accessible via cheat code (X+Y + button sequence) indicates developer difficulty acknowledgment
replayability_hooks: Multiple difficulty settings in international versions
Two-player cooperative play (implied by sequel features)
Hidden level select for direct stage access
Unused Stage 7 in development suggests extended content possibilities

world_building_techniques: Japanese mythology integration (yokai, shrine maiden lore, tanuki companion)
Environmental design reflecting spiritual/demonic themes (stained-glass murals in Stage 6, altered between JP/International versions)
Character-driven narrative (Pocky as protagonist, Rocky as companion, Black Mantle as antagonist)
Cutscene delivery (present in JP and US versions; completely removed in EU version for localization reasons)

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-heavy encounters with regional censorship considerations. Stage 4 boss redesigned from harpy (JP) to armored bird knight (International) suggesting content sensitivity in design iteration. Bosses serve as progression gates in linear stage structure
notable_bosses: Black Mantle (final antagonist, drives main narrative)
Stage 4 Boss: Harpy (JP version) / Bird Knight (International versions)
Implied goblin-derived enemies (corrupted goblins form enemy waves per narrative)

faction_indicators: Shrine maiden (Pocky) vs. corrupted spirit forces
Tanuki companion loyalty indicating yokai civilization complexity
Goblins corrupted by Black Mantle suggesting corruption/control dynamic
danger_signals: Black Mantle as mysterious villain of unknown origin/motivation
Widespread corruption of supernatural beings indicates large-scale supernatural crisis
Shrine maiden mobilization suggests spiritual emergency exceeding normal purification duties
environmental_storytelling: Stage 6 stained-glass murals depicting demonic/spiritual entities (censored in International versions)
Shrine/spiritual setting establishing Japanese supernatural cosmology
Progressive stage environments suggesting journey through spirit realms toward central antagonist

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Pocky & Rocky article (gameplay differences, regional variations, boss changes, difficulty mechanics)
Wikipedia - Pocky & Rocky series article (narrative context, character descriptions, developer information, series history)
